text: Year of the World 3405, Year before Christ 599.
Engravers. Hebrew, "those who enclose," (4 Kings 24:14.; 1 Kings 13:19.)
Season. Appearing in autumn and ripe in spring, (Micheas 7:1.) (Calmet)

Regard. Literally, "know," with love, (Psalm 1:6.) (Haydock)<hr>Jechonias was treated with honour, (4 Kings 25:27.) as well as Daniel and Zorobabel, of the same family. (Daniel 2:48.; Jeremiah 29:5.) The captives, under Sedecias, were much more abused. (Jeremiah 28:8.; Jeremiah 29:17.) (Calmet)<hr>This is the literal sense; the mystical shews that the good shall be rewarded. (Worthington)

Up, till the Messias appear, and the true Israel of God, his disciples, to whom the promises chiefly pertain. They will continue for ever. If this answer does not give satisfaction, as it ought, we may say that the Jews did not comply with the condition, and were therefore abandoned to the Romans. (Calmet)
Egypt, to which they fled contrary to the prophet's advice, (4 Kings 25:26.)
